web_results.title: Luka Doncic Will Finally Win NBA MVP In 2026 – The Lead
web_results.description: For him, winning the MVP award is a matter of “when,” not “if.” And with factors both on the court and in the voting ballots in his favor, he will finally receive his recognition early next year.

web_results.title: NBA insider questions if Lakers Luka Doncic will win his first MVP award in 2025-26 | Sporting News
web_results.description: During the 2024-25 season, he averaged 28.2 PPG, 8.2 RPG, 7.7 APG, 0.4 BPG, and 1.8 SPG. Doncic also shot 45.0% from the field. Hopefully he is ready to dominate the league in 2025-26.

web_results.title: NBA MVP odds 2025-26: Favorites to win - CBS Sports
web_results.description: The Thunder guard has a clear edge here, thanks in large part to Nikola Jokic's injury which is likely going to make the star center ineligible for the honor due to the 65-game threshold.

web_results.title: NBA MVP’s 10 best candidates for 2025-2026 season, ranked after Nikola Jokic’s injury | SB Nation
web_results.description: He’ll never be a defensive stopper, but it’s hard to imagine any player could be when they’re carrying a 39 percent usage rate that is twice as high as an average player. Doncic would play 75 games this season if he doesn’t miss another one, so he has a little margin for error on eligibility, too. This probably isn’t the year Luka finally wins his first MVP, but he’s one nagging injury away from probably getting it.

web_results.title: Projecting the 2025–26 NBA MVP Race: Luka Dončić Leads a Loaded Field
web_results.description: Nikola Jokic, Luka Doncic, and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander headline the race for the 2025-26 NBA MVP. Here’s our pick to win it.
